steps.title: Define the pound amounts
steps.content:
Let $p$ be pounds of peaches and $c$ be pounds of cucumbers. Lina bought twice as many pounds of cucumbers as pounds of peaches, so
$$
c=2p
$$

steps.title: Write the cost equation
steps.content:
Use the cost per pound for each food and the total cost.
$$
0.75p+1.10c=8.85
$$
Substitute $c=2p$.
$$
0.75p+1.10(2p)=8.85
$$

steps.title: Find the cucumber amount
steps.content: Solve for $p$, then use the relationship between the two amounts. $$ 0.75p+2.20p=8.85 $$ $$ 2.95p=8.85 $$ $$ p=3 $$ $$ c=2(3)=6 $$ The answer is D.
